sections.paragraph:
σκληρός, ά, όν (σκέλλομαι ‘be parched, be dry’; Hes., Hdt.+; ‘hard [to the touch], harsh’)

① pert. to being externally hard or rough, hard (to the touch), rough, of things λίθοι hard (OGI 194, 28; Wsd 11:4) Hs 9, 6, 8; 9, 8, 6ab. ῥάβδος rough, of a knotty stick (s. Pind., O. 7, 29; Diogenes the Cynic [IV b.c.] in Diog. L. 6, 21 σκληρὸν ξύλον=a hard staff; Aelian, VH 10, 16) 6, 2, 5.

② pert. to causing an adverse reaction because of being hard or harsh, hard, harsh, unpleasant, fig. ext. of 1, of words (Demetrius in Stob., Flor. 3, 8, 20 vol. III p. 345 H.; Diogenes, Ep. 21; Gen 21:11; 42:7; Dt 1:17; En) J 6:60; Jd 15 (after En 1:9); s. Hv 1, 4, 2 (w. χαλεπός). ἐντολαί hard, difficult (Diod S 14, 105, 2 σκ. πρόσταγμα; Porphyr., Vi. Pyth. 8 προστάγματα) Hm 12, 3, 4f; 12, 4, 4 (w. δύσβατος). ἄνεμοι rough, strong (Aelian, VH 9, 14; Pollux 1, 110; Procop., Bell. 3, 13, 5; Pr 27:16) Js 3:4.

③ pert. to being difficult to the point of being impossible, hard, implying an adverse force that is unyielding, the neut.: σκληρόν σοι (sc. ἐστίν) it is hard for you w. inf. foll. Ac 9:4 v.l., 6 v.l.; 26:14.

④ pert. to being unyielding in behavior or attitude

ⓐ of pers., in dealing with others hard, strict, harsh, cruel, merciless (Soph., Pla. et al.; OGI 194, 14; 1 Km 25:3; Is 19:4; 48:4; PsSol 4:2; EpArist 289; Mel., P. 20, 138) Mt 25:24. Of the devil Hm 12, 5, 1.

ⓑ in response to a call for change of mind, subst. τὸ σκληρόν stubbornness w. gen. (Polyb. 4, 21, 1; Jos., Ant. 16, 151 τὸ σκ. τοῦ τρόπου) τὸ σκ. τῆς καρδίας the hardness of heart B 9:5 v.l. (for σκληροκαρδία, q.v.).—On the history of the word s. KDieterich, RhM, n.s. 60, 1905, 236ff; FDanker, Hardness of Heart, CTM 44, ’73, 89–100, Deafness and Hearing in the Bible, in The Word in Signs and Wonders, ed. DPokorny/RHohenstein ’77, 25–37.—B. 1064.—DELG s.v. σκέλλομαι. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

ext. ext. = extension

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Hv Hv = Visions

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Hm Hm = Mandates

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

pers. pers. = person(s)

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

gen. gen. = genitive

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

RhM RhM = Rheinisches Museum für Philologie—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

CTM CTM = Concordia Theological Monthly, then CTM; s. also CurTM—List 6

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 930
